params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Favorite Colors data from Warmup. Red=40, Blue=30, Green=50, Yellow=20. Axis labeled 0 to 60 in intervals of 10. All 4 bars pre-filled.
Red bar highlights.
Horizontal guideline draws from top of Red bar to vertical axis at 40. The 40 mark on the axis glows.

beats.text: You saw this bar graph in Warmup. Now let's learn how to read it exactly.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Favorite Colors data. Red bar highlighted with guideline to axis at 40. Axis labeled 0–60 in 10s.

params.description: Axis labels 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60 highlight sequentially from bottom to top.

beats.text: See these numbers along the side? They show the scale. 10, 20, 30, 40. That's counting by 10s.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Red bar highlighted with guideline to axis at 40. Axis labels highlighted showing scale of 10.

beats.text: Red has 40. The height of the bar ends at the 40 line.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Red bar highlighted with guideline to axis at 40.

params.description: Axis labels 10, 20, 30, 40 pulse sequentially as counting animation plays.

beats.text: I can count by 10s to check: 10, 20, 30, 40. You already know how to count by 10s.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Red bar highlighted at 40. Counting animation complete.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3 — counted symbols instead of reading the axis value

params.description: Blue bar stays highlighted. Axis labels 10, 20, 30 pulse to draw attention to scale.

steps.text: You're thinking of the picture graph. On a bar graph, we read the axis. Where does the top of the Blue bar end?

incorrects.description: Student selected 20 — misread the axis line where the bar ends

params.description: Horizontal guideline draws from top of Blue bar to axis, landing at 30 mark.

steps.text: Follow the top of the Blue bar across to the axis. It ends at 30, not 20. Count the lines: 10, 20, 30.

incorrects.description: Student selected 35 — tried to estimate between lines when bar is at a line

params.description: Horizontal guideline draws from top of Blue bar to axis, landing exactly at 30 mark.

steps.text: The bar ends exactly at a line, not between lines. Follow it across. It lands right at 30.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Horizontal guideline draws from top of Blue bar to axis at 30. The 30 mark glows.

steps.text: I follow the top of the Blue bar across to the axis. It ends right at the 30 line. That's counting by 10s: 10, 20, 30. The bar shows 30 votes for Blue. That's how we read bar heights.

incorrects.description: Student selected 2, confusing the number of bars or marks with the value

params.description: Yellow bar remains highlighted. Axis marks 10 and 20 pulse to draw attention to the scale.

steps.text: You counted something, but not the value. Look at where the bar ends on the axis. The numbers on the side show the scale.

incorrects.description: Student selected 12, possibly adding 10 and 2 or misreading the scale

params.description: Axis marks pulse sequentially: 10, 20.

steps.text: The scale goes by 10s, not 1s. Count by 10s: 10, 20. Where does the Yellow bar end?

incorrects.description: Student selected 25, possibly estimating halfway between 20 and 30

params.description: Yellow bar remains highlighted. The 20 axis mark glows.

steps.text: Look closely at where the bar ends. It's right at the 20 line, not between lines. Count by 10s to check.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Axis marks 10, 20 pulse sequentially as counting animation plays for Yellow bar.

steps.text: Let me show you. I count by 10s up the axis: 10, 20. The Yellow bar ends right at the 20 line. The bar shows 20, not any other number. That's how the scale works.

incorrects.description: Student set Swings bar to wrong height on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Look at the data table. Swings has 30 votes. Find the 30 line on the axis.

incorrects.description: Student set Swings bar to wrong height on second attempt

params.description: The 30 mark on the axis highlights.

steps.text: Count by 10s up the axis: 10, 20, 30. Set the bar so it ends at the 30 line.

incorrects.description: Student set Swings bar to wrong height three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates setting Swings bar to height 30, bar fills from 0 to 30.

steps.text: Let me show you. I count by 10s: 10, 20, 30. I set the bar to end at the 30 line. The height of the bar shows the number of votes. Swings has 30 votes, so the bar ends at 30.

incorrects.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to wrong height on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Look at the data table. What number does Nonfiction show?

incorrects.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to wrong height on second attempt

params.description: The 60 line on the axis highlights.

steps.text: Nonfiction has 60 books. Find the 60 line on the axis. Count by 10s if you need to: 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, 60. Set the bar to end at that line.

incorrects.description: Student set Nonfiction bar to wrong height three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates setting Nonfiction bar to height 60, extending from 0 to the 60 line.

steps.text: Let me show you. The data table says Nonfiction has 60 books. I find the 60 line on the axis. Then I set the bar to end right there. The bar extends from 0 all the way to 60. That's how we show 60 books on a bar graph with scale of 10.

incorrects.description: Student selected 20, the value of Comics instead of the difference

params.description: Nonfiction and Comics bars highlight.

steps.text: You found Comics. But the question asks how many MORE. Find the difference between Nonfiction and Comics.

incorrects.description: Student selected 60, the value of Nonfiction instead of the difference

params.description: Nonfiction bar highlights.

steps.text: You found Nonfiction. But when we ask how many MORE, we need the difference. How far apart are Nonfiction and Comics?

incorrects.description: Student selected 80, the sum instead of the difference

params.description: Nonfiction and Comics bars highlight.

steps.text: You added the two values. But the question asks how many MORE. That means subtract: find the difference.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation shows Nonfiction bar at 60, Comics bar at 20, then the gap between them highlights to show the difference of 40.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. Nonfiction is at 60. Comics is at 20. To find how many MORE, we subtract: 60 minus 20 equals 40. The difference between them is 40.

params.title: Weekly Steps
params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Weekly Steps data. Mon=35, Tue=45, Wed=50, Thu=25, Fri=40. Scale of 10, y-axis 0–60.

beats.text: You can read bars that end right at the lines. But what if a bar ends between the lines?

elements.description: Vertical bar graph in reading mode. Weekly Steps data with five bars. Scale of 10, y-axis 0–60.
elements.title: Weekly Steps

params.title: Stickers Collected
params.description: Vertical bar graph appears. Stickers Collected. Emma=30 at 30 line, Jake=25 between 20–30 lines, Mia=40 at 40 line, Leo=35 between 30–40 lines. Scale of 10, axis 0–50. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible (lighter than 10s lines).
Jake's bar highlights.

beats.text: Look at Jake's bar. It doesn't end at a line. It ends between the lines.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Stickers Collected. Emma=30, Jake=25, Mia=40, Leo=35. Jake's bar highlighted. Scale of 10, axis 0–50. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

beats.text: Remember half-symbols from scale of 2? Half of 2 was 1. Same idea here. Half of 10 is 5.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Stickers Collected. Emma=30, Jake=25, Mia=40, Leo=35. Jake's bar highlighted. Scale of 10, axis 0–50. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

beats.text: Half of 10 is 5. So between 20 and 30, that's 20 plus 5, which equals 25. Jake has 25 stickers.

elements.description: Vertical bar graph. Stickers Collected. Emma=30, Jake=25, Mia=40, Leo=35. Jake's bar highlighted. Scale of 10, axis 0–50. Ghost gridlines at 5s visible.

incorrects.description: Student selected 30, which is the gridline below where Leo's bar ends

params.description: Leo's bar remains highlighted. The 30 and 40 gridlines pulse to show the range.

steps.text: Leo's bar goes higher than 30. It ends between 30 and 40. Where exactly does it stop?

incorrects.description: Student selected 40, which is the gridline above where Leo's bar ends

params.description: Leo's bar remains highlighted. The 40 gridline pulses, then the bar's endpoint highlights to show it stops below 40.

steps.text: Leo's bar doesn't reach all the way to 40. It stops between the lines. Look at where the bar ends.

incorrects.description: Student selected 34, close but not the halfway point between 30 and 40

params.description: Leo's bar remains highlighted. A ghost gridline appears at 35, showing the halfway point.

steps.text: Leo's bar ends right in the middle. Half of 10 is 5. So halfway between 30 and 40 is 30 plus 5.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Animation shows counting from 30: the 30 line glows, then a ghost gridline appears at 35 (halfway), then the 40 line glows. Leo's bar endpoint aligns with the 35 mark.

steps.text: Leo's bar ends halfway between 30 and 40. Half of 10 is 5. So 30 plus 5 equals 35. When a bar ends between the lines, we find the halfway point. That's how we read values between the gridlines.

incorrects.description: Student set Team A bar to wrong height on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Look between 40 and 50. Where is halfway?

incorrects.description: Student set Team A bar to wrong height on second attempt

params.description: 40 and 50 axis marks highlight to show the interval.

steps.text: Team A scored 45 points. Half of 10 is 5. So between 40 and 50, that's 40 plus 5. Set the bar at 45.

incorrects.description: Student set Team A bar to wrong height three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates setting Team A bar to height 45.

steps.text: Let me show you. Team A scored 45 points. I find 40 on the axis. Half of 10 is 5, so I go halfway up to 50. That's 45. The bar ends right in the middle. That's how we show 45 on a graph with scale of 10.

incorrects.description: Student set Runner C bar to wrong height on first attempt

steps.text: Look at the ghost gridlines. Runner C needs 35. Where is that between the lines?

incorrects.description: Student set Runner C bar to wrong height on second attempt

params.description: Axis marks at 30, 35, and 40 highlight. Ghost gridline at 35 pulses.

steps.text: 35 is halfway between 30 and 40. Half of 10 is 5, so 30 plus 5 equals 35. Set the bar to that height.

incorrects.description: Student set Runner C bar to wrong height three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates setting Runner C bar to height 35. Bar grows from 0 to 35, stopping at the ghost gridline halfway between 30 and 40.

steps.text: Let me show you. Runner C completed 35 laps. I find 30 on the axis, then 40. Halfway between them is 35. The bar ends right there at the middle line. That's how you place a bar between the axis lines.

incorrects.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Look at the data table. Which numbers end in 0 or 5?

incorrects.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly on second attempt

params.description: Monday and Wednesday bars highlight to show which values are at axis lines.

steps.text: Monday is 40 and Wednesday is 50. Those end in 0, so they're right at the axis lines. Tuesday is 35 and Thursday is 25. Those end in 5, so they're halfway between. Set each bar where it belongs.

incorrects.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates setting all four bars to correct heights: Monday=40, Tuesday=35, Wednesday=50, Thursday=25.

steps.text: Let me show you. Monday is 40, right at the 40 line. Tuesday is 35, halfway between 30 and 40. Wednesday is 50, at the 50 line. Thursday is 25, halfway between 20 and 30. Numbers ending in 0 are at the lines. Numbers ending in 5 are between them.

incorrects.description: Student selected 35: chose only Tuesday's value instead of adding both days

params.description: Monday and Tuesday bars highlight.

steps.text: You found Tuesday's value. The question asks for TOTAL minutes. That means add Monday and Tuesday together.

incorrects.description: Student selected 40: chose only Monday's value instead of adding both days

params.description: Monday and Tuesday bars highlight.

steps.text: You found Monday's value. The question asks for the TOTAL. That means add Monday and Tuesday together. What is 40 plus 35?

incorrects.description: Student selected 85: added Monday and Wednesday, or made a calculation error

params.description: Monday and Tuesday bars highlight.

steps.text: Check which days the question asks about. Monday is 40, Tuesday is 35. Add those two values. What is 40 plus 35?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Monday bar glows, then Tuesday bar glows. Values 40 and 35 appear and combine to show 75.

steps.text: Let me show you. Monday's bar ends at 40. Tuesday's bar ends at 35. The question asks for the TOTAL, so we add them. 40 plus 35 equals 75. The answer is 75 minutes total.

params.title: Minutes of Reading
params.description: Horizontal bar graph appears. Minutes of Reading data. Monday=40, Tuesday=35, Wednesday=50, Thursday=25. X-axis labeled 0–60 in intervals of 10.

beats.text: You've made vertical bar graphs. Now let's try one that goes sideways. Horizontal.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph in reading mode. Minutes of Reading data. Four categories (Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday) with values 40, 35, 50, 25. X-axis scaled 0–60 by 10s.
elements.title: Minutes of Reading

incorrects.description: Student selected 3, counting symbols or confusing value with number of categories

params.description: Museum bar highlights.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. The Museum bar ends at a line on the axis. Look at the numbers along the bottom. Where does the bar end?

incorrects.description: Student selected 25, estimating between 20 and 30 but missing the exact line

params.description: Museum bar highlights with axis marks visible.

steps.text: You're working on it. Museum's bar ends right at a line, not between the lines. Check which axis line the bar touches.

incorrects.description: Student selected 35, reading the wrong bar or confusing axis values

params.description: Museum bar highlights clearly.

steps.text: Here's a hint: Find the Museum bar. Follow it to the right until it ends. What number is at that point on the scale?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Guideline draws from end of Museum bar down to axis at 30. The 30 mark glows.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. I find the Museum bar. I follow it to the right. It ends exactly at the 30 line on the axis. The Museum bar shows 30. When a bar ends right at a line, that line tells us the value.

incorrects.description: Student selected 50, the lower gridline endpoint

params.description: Park bar remains highlighted. Ghost gridline at 50 pulses to show proximity.

steps.text: The bar goes PAST 50. Look at where it ends. Is it at the 50 line or between the lines?

incorrects.description: Student selected 60, the upper gridline endpoint

params.description: Park bar remains highlighted. Ghost gridline at 60 pulses to show the bar ends before it.

steps.text: The bar does not reach 60. Look at where the bar actually ends. Count from 50.

incorrects.description: Student selected 58, a value between the gridlines but not halfway

params.description: Park bar remains highlighted. Ghost gridlines at 50 and 60 pulse to show the interval.

steps.text: The bar is between 50 and 60, but where exactly? Half of 10 is 5. Halfway between 50 and 60 is 55.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Horizontal guideline draws from end of Park bar down to axis at 55. The ghost gridline at 55 glows to confirm position.

steps.text: Let me show you how to find it. The bar ends between 50 and 60. That means we count by 10s: 50, then halfway to 60 is 55. The bar ends at 55 because it's right in the middle between the two lines.

incorrects.description: Student placed one or more bars incorrectly on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Check the data table and look at the axis lines carefully.

incorrects.description: Student placed one or more bars incorrectly on second attempt

params.description: Ava bar position highlights on axis at 50.

steps.text: Ava walked 50 miles. That's right at the 50 line. The others are between lines. Find where each bar ends by counting by 10s, then add 5 if it's halfway.

incorrects.description: Student placed one or more bars incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates placing each bar: Kai at 35 (between 30 and 40), Ava at 50 (at the line), Noah at 25 (between 20 and 30), Zoe at 45 (between 40 and 50).

steps.text: Let me show you. Kai walked 35 miles. That's between 30 and 40, halfway. Ava walked 50, right at the 50 line. Noah walked 25, halfway between 20 and 30. Zoe walked 45, halfway between 40 and 50. When a value ends in 5, it's always halfway between two lines on a scale of 10.

incorrects.description: Student selected 15, incorrect. May have subtracted 10 from 25 or miscalculated the difference.

params.description: Ava and Noah bars highlight.

steps.text: Find Ava's bar and Noah's bar. Read where each one ends on the scale. Then find how far apart they are.

incorrects.description: Student selected 50, incorrect. Gave Ava's total instead of the difference.

params.description: Ava and Noah bars highlight.

steps.text: You found Ava's total. The question asks how many MORE. That means find the difference between Ava and Noah.

incorrects.description: Student selected 75, incorrect. Added Ava and Noah instead of finding the difference.

params.description: Ava and Noah bars highlight.

steps.text: You added them. The question asks how many MORE. That means subtract to find the difference.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: The portion of Ava's bar that extends beyond Noah's bar highlights, showing the 25-unit difference between 50 and 25.

steps.text: Let me show you how to find the difference. Ava's bar ends at 50. Noah's bar ends at 25. To find how many MORE, I subtract: 50 minus 25 equals 25. The answer is 25 more miles.

incorrects.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly on first attempt

steps.text: Check the data table. Make sure each bar matches the number.

incorrects.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly on second attempt

params.description: All categories highlight in data table.

steps.text: Look at the ghost gridlines. They help you see where halfway is. Tomatoes is 60, right at the line. Peppers is 35, halfway between 30 and 40. Find where each number goes.

incorrects.description: Student set one or more bars incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System demonstrates setting each bar sequentially: Tomatoes to 60, Peppers to 35, Carrots to 45, Beans to 20.

steps.text: Here's how it works. Tomatoes is 60, at the 60 line. Peppers is 35, halfway between 30 and 40. Carrots is 45, halfway between 40 and 50. Beans is 20, right at the 20 line. When you read the data table, you find each value on the axis and set the bar there.

incorrects.description: Student selected 70, possibly reading one category value instead of adding

params.description: Peppers and Carrots bars highlight.

steps.text: The question asks for the total. Find both values on the graph, then add them together. What is 35 plus 45?

incorrects.description: Student selected 75, close but incorrect sum

params.description: Peppers and Carrots bars highlight.

steps.text: Check your addition. Peppers is at 35, Carrots is at 45. Add those two numbers carefully.

incorrects.description: Student selected 90, possible counting error or misreading bars

params.description: Peppers and Carrots bars highlight.

steps.text: Read each bar carefully. Peppers ends at 35, halfway between 30 and 40. Carrots ends at 45, halfway between 40 and 50. Now add those.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: System traces Peppers bar to 35 on axis, then Carrots bar to 45 on axis. Both values glow.

steps.text: Let me show you. Peppers is at 35. Carrots is at 45. To find the total, I add 35 plus 45. That equals 80. The answer is 80 plants in all.

params.title: Plants in the Garden
params.description: Horizontal bar graph titled Plants in the Garden. Four bars: Tomatoes=60, Peppers=35, Carrots=45, Beans=20. Scale of 10, axis range 0–70.

beats.text: You made bar graphs with scale of 10. You used the axis to find values, even values between the lines. Let's see what you know.

elements.description: Horizontal bar graph titled Plants in the Garden with four completed bars: Tomatoes=60, Peppers=35, Carrots=45, Beans=20. Scale of 10, axis range 0–70.
